meta.dc:title: 119 HR 2215 ENR: To redesignate the Salem Maritime National Historic Site as the “Salem Maritime National Historical Park”, and for other purposes.
meta.dc:creator: United States House of Representatives
meta.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

preface.congress: One Hundred Nineteenth Congress of the United States of America
preface.session: A T T H E F I R S T S E S S I O N
preface.enrolledDateline: Begun and held at the City of Washington on Friday, the third day of January, two thousand and twenty-five

longTitle.officialTitle: To redesignate the Salem Maritime National Historic Site as the “Salem Maritime National Historical Park”, and for other purposes.

enactingFormula.i: Be it enacted by the Senate and House of Representa­tives of the United States of America in Congress assembled,

section.heading: SHORT TITLE.
section.content: This Act may be cited as the “Salem Maritime National Historical Park Redesignation and Boundary Study Act”.

subsection.heading: Redesignation.—
subsection.content: The Salem Maritime National Historic Site shall hereafter be known and designated as the “Salem Maritime National Historical Park”.

subsection.heading: References in Law.—
subsection.content: Any reference to the Salem Maritime National Historic Site in any law, regulation, map, document, record, or other paper of the United States shall be deemed to be a reference to the Salem Maritime National Historical Park.

subsection.heading: In General.—
subsection.chapeau: The Secretary shall conduct a boundary study to evaluate the suitability and feasibility of including in the National Park System, as part of the Salem Maritime National Historical Park (as redesignated by section 2(a)), sites and resources in the study area associated with the study area’s—

paragraph.content: military history, including National Guard and militia activity.

subsection.heading: Report.—
subsection.chapeau: Not later than 3 years after the date on which funds are made available to conduct the study required under subsection (a), the Secretary shall submit to the Committee on Natural Resources of the House of Representatives and the Committee on Energy and Natural Resources of the Senate a report containing—

paragraph.content: the results of the study; and
any findings, conclusions, and recommendations of the Secretary.

paragraph.heading: Secretary.—
paragraph.content: The term “Secretary” means the Secretary of the Interior.

paragraph.heading: Study area.—
paragraph.content: The term “study area” means Salem, Massachusetts, and its vicinity, and includes the Salem Armory Visitor Center building and adjacent Salem Armory Park.

signature.role: Speaker of the House of Representatives.
Vice President of the United States and President of the Senate.
